tdl-mailhes.note: Individual restaurant reviews; 782 total published articles confirmed; article_url is author index page; individual article URLs in each restaurant entry
tdl-mailhes.writer_profile: A food writer at Tribune de Lyon, François Mailhes covers the culinary scene across France's gastronomic capital. Tribune de Lyon provides local news and cultural coverage for the Lyon metropolitan area. His recent work includes comprehensive food guides highlighting the city's diverse restaurant landscape.

timeout-lyon.writer_profile: Food writer at Time Out, Anna Richards specializes in comprehensive dining guides that help readers navigate the best culinary experiences in their cities. Her recent food guide work for Time Out demonstrates her expertise in curating restaurant recommendations and food scene insights for urban diners.

signeture-living.note: Single-author personal blog; About page confirms Signe Meirane (also known as Signe Vanaga) as sole author and Lyon resident; single-author site rule applied
signeture-living.writer_profile: A contributor to Signeture Living, Signe Meirane writes comprehensive food guides that help readers navigate dining experiences. Her work focuses on curating culinary recommendations and insights for the publication's lifestyle-focused audience.

infatuation-lyon.note: 24 raw extractions; 4 excluded (Michelin starred); 7 excluded (concentration cap, brought publisher share to ~30%); Anna Richards also writes for Time Out — confirmed independently produced, qualifies for both-pool
infatuation-lyon.writer_profile: Food writer at The Infatuation, Anna Richards contributes to comprehensive dining guides that help readers discover noteworthy restaurants and eateries. The Infatuation specializes in accessible restaurant recommendations and food culture coverage across various cities and neighborhoods.

restaurants.name: Ayla
notable_dishes: sepia with beef marrow and wild garlic
restaurants.quote: Franco-Lebanese small-plates restaurant from chefs Najem Atmeh and Corinne Bec; seasonal produce in an open kitchen with tempura vine leaves on labneh and sepia with beef marrow; extensive French and Lebanese wine selection

restaurants.name: Kitchen
notable_dishes: fermented tomato ceviche of red sea bream
beet with citron, star anise, and trout roe
salsify with celery and wild garlic
restaurants.quote: Intimate bistro from chef Connie Zagora built around fermentation and locavore produce; daily changing menu open Friday to Monday for lunch and brunch; fermented tomato ceviche of red sea bream is a signature

restaurants.name: Daniel et Denise
restaurants.quote: Lyon bouchon institution since 1968 with four city locations; world-champion pâté en croûte (2009) alongside classic Lyonnais specialties including calf head and Bresse chicken

restaurants.name: L'Établi
restaurants.quote: Refined seasonal set-menu restaurant in the 2nd arrondissement; three, six, or seven-course menus with varied textures; carpenter's bench-themed tables and confit egg yolk and squid ink risotto among the standouts

restaurants.name: Amicus
notable_dishes: mackerel in escabeche with pickled radishes, fennel, shallots, cauliflower
black pollock with confit celery root and pastis sauce
vanilla cremeux with mascarpone and citrus marmalade
restaurants.quote: Neighbourhood bistro near the courthouse from chef Clément; refined seasonal plates with mackerel in escabeche praised as exalted; black pollock with pastis sauce and vanilla cremeux with citrus marmalade

restaurants.name: La Famille
restaurants.quote: Croix-Rousse bistro from chef Alexandre Gauthier delivering craftsman's cooking; veal breast, pâté-croûte, and skrei with crayfish bisque among the highlights

restaurants.name: Bonbec
restaurants.quote: Cheerful Part-Dieu bistro playing affectionately with French classics; œufs mayo and poireaux vinaigrette executed with care alongside choux with moules and chorizo

restaurants.name: Cinq mains
notable_dishes: roasted sucrine lettuce with hollandaise and yeast powder
pork chop with shallot compote and crushed potatoes
restaurants.quote: Vieux-Lyon bistro from Michelin Bib Gourmand chef Wandrille Beaugrand; thyme pita with fennel caviar and pork chop with shallot compote; lunch from €28, dinner from €41

restaurants.name: Kuro
notable_dishes: foie gras with sake and onion-ginger-citrus chutney
duck filet glazed with hoisin sauce
restaurants.quote: Brotteaux restaurant from Lyon-born David Siong (Chinese heritage) and Peka Sourignavong (Laotian heritage); joyful open kitchen with foie gras with sake and Norwegian salmon gravlax with miso

restaurants.name: Le Petit Abuluzi
notable_dishes: ravioles salad with beetroot, edamame, cashews, chorizo
restaurants.quote: Presqu'île restaurant from chef Migmar (ex-Paul Bocuse) serving Tibetan momos alongside French bistro dishes; beef, seafood, and vegetarian momos available alongside Charolais entrecôte with Tibetan morels

restaurants.name: L'Osteria Saint-Nizier
restaurants.flag: chain-origin (German parent company noted by Mailhes)
restaurants.quote: Place Saint-Nizier Italian with outsized bicycle-wheel-diameter pizzas and straightforward pasta; vitello tonnato and salsiccia piccante pizza; open daily until midnight on weekends

restaurants.name: Majorelle
notable_dishes: egg with mushrooms, truffle cream, and bacon
foie gras with corn bread and vanilla parsnip purée
scallops with black miso-citron sauce and lobster espuma
restaurants.quote: Part-Dieu bistronomic restaurant from chef Benjamin with a monthly seasonal menu; ambitious dessert programme including scallops with black miso-citron sauce; mixed review from Mailhes (2.5/4)

restaurants.name: Café Terroir
restaurants.quote: Very local, typical Lyonnais food including tartare, lentils with sausage, snails, and whole baked chicken; a neighbourhood institution recommended by Lyon resident Signe Meirane

restaurants.name: Breizh
restaurants.quote: Simple Brittany-style galettes and crêpes served all day with a generous variety of fillings; a reliable casual address

restaurants.name: Bel Ami
restaurants.quote: Simple sharing plates with hummus, sausages, and octopus alongside a local wine selection; relaxed atmosphere

restaurants.name: Madamann
restaurants.quote: Organic and local produce crêperie inside the Halles de la Martinière; a popular choice for weekend brunch in the 1st arrondissement

restaurants.name: Le Troquet
restaurants.quote: Local and organic produce restaurant with a frequently changing menu of shareable seasonal plates; a neighbourhood favourite

restaurants.name: Chez Pimmouse
restaurants.quote: Romantic restaurant on the Saône riverbank; light and elegant, recommended by Signe Meirane for special occasions

restaurants.name: Le Bistrot du Potager Stalingrad
restaurants.quote: Local neighbourhood gathering spot on the 6th–Villeurbanne border emphasising grilled meat and seasonal produce

restaurants.name: Le Bistrot Gerland
restaurants.quote: More elegant sister location of Le Bistrot du Potager in Gerland; modern, lighter interior with a seasonal menu

restaurants.name: Casareccio
restaurants.quote: City centre sourdough pizza with simple, very delicious toppings; accessible on foot from the centre of Lyon

restaurants.name: Leptine
restaurants.quote: Reservation-recommended restaurant blending French and Asian culinary traditions; on the border of the 1st and 2nd arrondissements

restaurants.name: Junk
restaurants.quote: Smash burgers and crispy fried chicken; a local favourite for American-style fast food recommended by Lyon resident Signe Meirane

restaurants.name: Le Bistrot d'Abel
restaurants.quote: Classic heavy Lyonnaise cuisine reinterpreted in a lighter, modern bistro style; notable for Lyonnaise salad and veal liver

restaurants.name: La Maison Cobalte
restaurants.quote: Restaurant, café, and bar hybrid in the 1st arrondissement serving classic-with-a-twist dishes and an extensive dessert selection

restaurants.name: Maria
restaurants.quote: Wood-fired Neapolitan pizza in the 1st arrondissement; eight varieties on the menu plus weekly rotating specials

restaurants.name: Sapnà
restaurants.quote: Globe-trotting menu with smoked trout gyoza, lamb satay bao, and an on-site pastry bar in the 1st arrondissement

restaurants.name: Söma
restaurants.quote: Minimalist Vieux-Lyon restaurant with a seasonal menu; tempura dates and cauliflower tabbouleh among the signature dishes

restaurants.name: Boleh Lah
restaurants.quote: Malaysian mamak street food in the 7th; roti flatbread, beef rendang, and nasi goreng; mains from €14

restaurants.name: Canard de Rue
restaurants.quote: Duck-themed restaurant in the 3rd serving duck burgers and duck hot dogs in a casual bar atmosphere; from €15.50

restaurants.name: Groom
restaurants.quote: Basement neon cocktail den in the 1st with craft spirits, charcuterie boards, and live music nights; cocktails from €11

restaurants.name: La Madone
restaurants.quote: Former rectory in the 1st converted into a relaxed bar serving craft beer, cocktails, and cheese and charcuterie boards; tapas from €6

restaurants.name: Circle
restaurants.quote: Creative tasting menu restaurant in the 1st with seasonal and international-influenced preparations; six or eight courses with okonomiyaki-hazelnut satay sauce and deconstructed makroud

restaurants.name: Alebrije
notable_dishes: guava and root vegetables in mole
restaurants.quote: Mexican restaurant balancing hearty classics with beautiful presentation; tacos with edible flowers, guava in mole, and spicy margaritas

restaurants.name: Chez Paul
restaurants.quote: Classic Lyon bouchon with long communal tables, a boisterous atmosphere, and sharing wine carafes; tripe and andouillette on the menu

restaurants.name: Becbec
restaurants.quote: Affordable neighbourhood vegetarian wine bar with hearty portions; kimchi cheese toasties, patatas bravas, and cheese platters in a casual artsy setting

restaurants.name: Brasserie Georges
restaurants.quote: Lyon brasserie institution since 1836 with Art Deco chandeliers and a celebratory atmosphere; a landmark address for traditional French brasserie classics

restaurants.name: Nafas
restaurants.quote: Refugee-run Syrian restaurant with generous portions at low prices and a sprawling terrace; falafel, shawarma, and diverse dips under €13

restaurants.name: Regain
restaurants.quote: Beige cellar-like dining room with beautifully presented seasonal plates; lamb tartare, barbecued squid, and rose-infused meringue; set menus €25–€75

restaurants.name: Les Muses de l'Opéra
restaurants.neighbourhood_hint: rooftop of Opéra de Lyon, 1st arrondissement
restaurants.quote: Seasonal rooftop terrace atop the Opéra de Lyon open May to September; the city's best sunset views with charcuterie, truffle sausage, and eggplant caviar

restaurants.name: Les Mauvaises Herbes
restaurants.quote: Split-level plant-filled vegetarian fine dining restaurant open since 2018; won over both vegetarians and carnivores; zucchini-pomelo ceviche and romaine leaf tacos

restaurants.name: Flair
restaurants.quote: Value-focused French-Japanese fusion in the 2nd; parsnip three ways, foie gras with bamboo-tofu sauce, and grilled octopus in ponzu; lunch from €27

restaurants.name: Les Marmottes
restaurants.quote: Chalet-aesthetic Savoyard restaurant with all-you-can-eat raclette; wild garlic fondue and tartiflette with boletus mushrooms in a wood-panelled warm setting

run_metrics.publisher_concentration_flag: FR pool: Tribune de Lyon 100% (structural gap — no other named FR food critics identified)
